title: Undulate, My Tongue
content:
My tongue undulates, a wave to shore,
    knocks a vigorous reef,
    then slides to sea once more.

    The coral pink horizon of the mouth,
    cavernous shores,
    my tongue laps pier white teeth
    in servitude like an oar.


    Heavy drifting, bobbing as a buoy,
    the tongue sinks slowly down before
    surprising saliva
    going ashore.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Paul Cameron Brown delivers a powerful performance in "Undulate, My Tongue"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
